data.title: Fidus Investment reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Fidus Investment press release (NASDAQ:FDUS): Q2 Total investment income of $40.0 million
  • Net investment income of $18.6 million, or $0.53 per share
  • Adjusted net investment income of $20.0 million, or $0.57 per share(1)
  • Invested $94.5 million in debt and equity securities, including four new portfolio companies
  • Received proceeds from repayments and realizations of $109.3 million
  • Paid total dividends of $0.54 per share: regular quarterly dividend of $0.43 and a supplemental dividend of $0.11 per share on June 25, 2025
  • Net asset value (“NAV”) of $692.3 million, or $19.57 per share, as of June 30, 2025
  • Estimated spillover income (or taxable income in excess of distributions) as of June 30, 2025 of $49.0 million, or $1.39 per share
  • Shares +2.92%.

data.title: ETF power plays: High performance funds with structural strength
data.description:
While exchange traded funds are generally structured to provide diversification through broad exposure to a variety of assets, not all adhere to this traditional model.

A growing number of ETFs now concentrate on specific sectors or maintain narrowly focused portfolios with high-conviction allocations, or are even heavily leveraged. However, select funds continue to embody the traditional core principles of diversification—offering broad market exposure, reduced concentration risk, and the potential for sustainable long-term growth.

In 2025, the S&P 500 (SP500) has delivered a year-to-date return of approximately 7.5%. In contrast, the ETFs highlighted below have each generated returns of at least 15% or more, outperforming the broader market by a significant margin.

These funds are not only delivering strong performance but also stand out for their structural robustness. Each ETF on the list has more than $25B in assets under management, holds at least 250 individual securities, and limits the weight of its top 10 holdings to no more than 17.5% of the total portfolio—ensuring a high degree of diversification and liquidity for investors.

Listed below are the 10 ETFs that fit this mold ranked by year-to-date performance.

No. 1: iShares MSCI EAFE Value ETF (BATS:EFV), +23.2% YTD.

No. 2: Vanguard FTSE Europe ETF (NYSEARCA:VGK), +20.6% YTD.

No. 3: Schwab International Equity ETF (NYSEARCA:SCHF), +20% YTD.

No. 4: Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets Index Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:VEA), +19.9% YTD.

No. 5: SPDR Portfolio Developed World ex-US ETF (NYSEARCA:SPDW), +19.3% YTD.

No. 6: iShares Core MSCI EAFE ETF (BATS:IEFA), +18.6% YTD.

No. 7: Vanguard Total International Stock Index Fund ETF (NASDAQ:VXUS), +18.2% YTD.

No. 8: Vanguard FTSE All-World ex-US Index Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:VEU), +18% YTD.

No. 9: iShares Core MSCI Total International Stock ETF (NASDAQ:IXUS), +17.9% YTD.

No. 10: iShares MSCI EAFE ETF (NYSEARCA:EFA), +17.7% YTD.

data.title: Trump set to nominate adviser Miran for 4-month Fed governor term - report
data.description:
President Trump is poised to nominate Stephen Miran, chairman of the Council of Economic Advisers, to serve as a Federal Reserve governor, according to a media report on Thursday.

On Wednesday, the president said he'd likely nominate a temporary governor to fill the seat, rather than someone who would eventually be elevated to Fed chair. Miran, if nominated and approved by the Senate, is only expected to serve the remainder of Adriana Kugler's term, which is set to expire in January, Bloomberg reported, citing a person familiar with the matter.

Last week, Kugler said she'd step down from the Fed board as of Aug. 8 to resume her professorship at Georgetown University.

The White House said a decision won't be final until formally announced by Trump, Bloomberg said.

"Unless it comes from President Trump himself, however, any discussion about personnel decisions should be regarded as pure speculation," White House spokesman Kush Desai told Bloomberg.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
